terms.definition: In this lesson, a provisional judgment supported by a pattern of successful unaided performance after delay and variation under declared conditions; it is not a universal threshold or a professional credential.
Observable reconstruction or use of knowledge after a meaningful delay, under stated conditions, rather than only during the original study encounter.
A subjective sense that material is recognizable or easy while it is present; by itself, it does not show that the learner can reconstruct or use the material later.
An attempt to generate a relevant answer, explanation, prediction, representation, or procedure from memory before consulting the answer source.
Distributing learning or retrieval opportunities across time instead of placing all opportunities in one uninterrupted session.
The future period over which the learner needs to retain and use the target knowledge, used here to constrain a retrieval plan.
Information after an attempt that identifies what was correct or incorrect, explains the relevant principle or process, and directs a useful next action.
Mixing already introduced categories or strategies so that the learner must select and discriminate among them rather than repeat one type in a block.
A predeclared link between observed performance and the next planning action, such as adding support after a missing relation or extending a gap after successful delayed performance.
